description: Migration: creating `contact_phone_type_definitions` enforces tenant-scoped uniqueness on normalized custom labels.
Migration: creating `contact_phone_numbers` enforces that exactly one of `canonical_type` or `custom_phone_type_id` is set.
Migration: `contact_phone_numbers` enforces at most one default phone number per contact.
Migration: backfill converts an existing scalar `contacts.phone_number` into one default `work` phone row for the same contact.
Migration: backfill leaves contacts with null/empty scalar phone values with zero normalized phone rows.
DB-backed integration: normalized phone values are stored in searchable form independently of punctuation/spacing in the display phone string.
Type/API: shared contact types reject legacy scalar-only contact payloads and accept `phone_numbers` collections.
Validation: contact create rejects a phone collection with two rows both marked as default.
Validation: contact create rejects a phone collection with numbers present but no default row selected.
Validation: contact create accepts canonical phone rows and one custom-type row in the same payload.
Validation: contact update reuses an existing tenant custom phone type definition when the same label is entered with different case or spacing.
DB-backed integration: creating a contact with multiple phone rows persists the parent contact and ordered child phone rows in one transaction.
DB-backed integration: updating a contact can replace, reorder, and re-default child phone rows without leaving orphaned or duplicate rows behind.
DB-backed integration: a failed contact phone write rolls back both parent and child mutations.
Read-path integration: fetching a contact returns `phone_numbers` ordered by `display_order` and exposes the correct default row.
UI integration: `ContactDetails.tsx` lets a user add a second phone row, assign a custom type, switch the default row, and save successfully.
UI validation: `ContactDetails.tsx` prevents save when multiple phone rows are marked default.
UI integration: `ContactDetailsEdit.tsx` renders existing multiple phone rows and preserves custom type labels on reopen.
UI regression: `ContactDetailsView.tsx` shows the derived default phone instead of expecting scalar `phone_number`.
UI integration: `QuickAddContact.tsx` can create a contact with one default canonical phone and one additional phone row.
UI integration: `QuickAddClient.tsx` creates the inline contact with the normalized phone collection and persists the chosen default phone.
UI regression: contacts list rows render the default phone number when a contact has multiple phone rows.
UI regression: client-scoped contacts list rows render the default phone number when a contact has multiple phone rows.
Search integration: contact search by a secondary non-default phone number returns the contact.
Sorting integration: contact list phone sorting uses the default phone number rather than an arbitrary child row.
UI regression: `TicketProperties.tsx` shows the default contact phone when the ticket has a contact with multiple phone rows.
CSV import integration: importing one phone column creates one default normalized contact phone row.
CSV export integration: exporting contacts emits the derived default phone number rather than expecting `contacts.phone_number`.
UI copy regression: `ContactsImportDialog.tsx` preview/help text reflects the normalized model and default-phone export/import rule.
API schema regression: contact create/update endpoints validate `phone_numbers` collections and no longer require scalar `phone_number`.
Workflow event regression: `CONTACT_CREATED` payloads emit the new phone data shape rather than scalar `phoneNumber` only.
Workflow event regression: `CONTACT_UPDATED` change payloads capture additions, removals, and default changes in the normalized phone model.
Integration: Entra sync maps `mobilePhone` to canonical `mobile` and preserves at least one `businessPhones[]` entry as canonical `work`.
Integration: when Entra supplies both mobile and business phones, the chosen default matches the agreed precedence rule.
Regression: contact test factories and seed helpers can create contacts with normalized phone rows without writing `contacts.phone_number` directly.
DB-backed integration: after application cutover, contact create/update/read flows succeed without reading or writing `contacts.phone_number`.
Migration: after Migration B, the schema no longer contains `contacts.phone_number` and the migrated application test suite still passes for contact flows.
